text.title: Agent Observability Stack Blueprint Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Agent Observability Stack Blueprint application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Instrument with OpenTelemetry GenAI semconv as the bottom layer. Vendor SDKs sit on top of OTel, not instead of it.
rule.rationale: OTel is the only contract that survives a vendor swap; vendor-only instrumentation forces full reinstrument on switch.

rule.statement: Capture all four: traces (call tree + I/O), judgments (LLM-judge or human labels), costs (tokens × price), trajectories (multi-step state). Skipping one is a blind spot.
rule.rationale: Industry case studies show one missing data type produces 30-50% drop in time-to-diagnose.

rule.statement: Pick exactly one vendor: LangSmith (LangChain shops), Langfuse (open-source self-host), Helicone (proxy-style), Phoenix Arize (multimodal/eval). Axes: cost-tier × self-host × framework.
rule.rationale: Multi-vendor stacks duplicate data, double cost, dilute eval set; one vendor + OTel is the proven pattern.

rule.statement: Wire per-day cost alert at 2× rolling-7-day baseline. Page on breach.
rule.rationale: Most agent-cost incidents are 4-10× budgets within hours; per-day alert catches before billing day.

rule.statement: Drift dashboard reviewed weekly: judgment-score-by-day, tool-call distribution, latency p95, cost-per-conversation. Five tracked metrics max.
rule.rationale: Dashboards >10 widgets are abandoned in <60 days; 5-metric weekly is the sustainable floor.
